name: Medical Air
description: MCL supplies pharmaceutical-grade medical compressed air for respiratory therapy, anaesthesia delivery, surgical instrument driving, and NICU applications. Available as cylinder supply or via our MCL-installed Medical Compressed Air Plant, conforming to European Pharmacopeia and HTM 02-01 standards.

use_cases.title: Respiratory Therapy
use_cases.description: Medical air is blended with oxygen in precise concentrations to deliver controlled FiO₂ to ventilator-dependent and spontaneously breathing patients, managing conditions from COPD to ARDS.

use_cases.title: Anaesthesia Delivery
use_cases.description: Used as the carrier gas in anaesthetic breathing circuits, medical air dilutes volatile anaesthetic agents and provides a controlled, non-enriched oxygen environment during surgery.

use_cases.title: Surgical Instrument Driving
use_cases.description: 700 kPa surgical air powers pneumatic drills, saws, and orthopaedic tools in theatres — eliminating the fire risk of oxygen-enriched atmospheres around powered instruments.

use_cases.title: NICU & Neonatal Care
use_cases.description: Precise oxygen-air blending in neonatal intensive care prevents hyperoxia and retinopathy of prematurity, where exact FiO₂ control is clinically critical.

use_cases.title: Mechanical Ventilation
use_cases.description: ICU ventilators require a continuous, high-quality medical air supply at consistent pressure and purity to deliver accurate tidal volumes and support long-term ventilated patients.

use_cases.title: Pulmonary Function Labs
use_cases.description: Calibration of spirometers and body plethysmographs requires traceable, certified medical air for accurate diagnostic pulmonary function testing.

Medical compressed air is a mixture of nitrogen and oxygen. It must never be confused with industrial air — oil, moisture, and contaminant limits are far tighter for patient-contact use.

PIPELINE SUPPLY
MEDICAL COMPRESSED
AIR PLANT (MCP)

For hospitals and healthcare facilities with continuous demand, MCL designs, supplies, installs, and commissions a Medical Compressed Air Plant — eliminating cylinder logistics and ensuring an uninterrupted, centralised supply throughout your facility. The plant is supplied as a complete package: pre-wired, piped, and factory-tested before shipping.

KEY FEATURES
Air Compressor Plant
Oil-free
Zero lubricant carry-over into the gas stream
Duplex
Duty/standby configuration for 100% uptime
Pre-wired
Factory-tested before shipping to site
HTM 02-01
Fully compliant design and documentation
Dew-pt alarm
Integrated moisture monitoring and alert
NFPA 99
Suitable for US-standard hospital projects

Models available with multiple compressor and pressure vessel combinations. Layout modified to fit irregular plant rooms.

SAFE USE
Handling & Storage
Medical air is not industrial air
Never substitute industrial compressed air for medical use — lubricant carry-over, moisture, and contaminants make it unsafe for patient contact.
Pipeline supply preferred for wards
MCL's MGPS team can design and install a centralised medical air plant for your facility, eliminating cylinder logistics in high-consumption environments.
Oil-free compressor mandatory
All medical air compressors must be oil-free. Our MCPs (Medical Compressed Air Plants) use oil-free piston or scroll compressor technology.
Monitor dew point continuously
A dew point above –46 °C at the pipeline indicates a moisture ingress problem. Continuous area alarms should be installed per HTM 02-01.
Colour code: Black body / white shoulders
MCL medical air cylinders are painted with black body and white-quartered shoulders per international medical gas cylinder standards.

name: Medical Carbon Dioxide
description: MCL supplies pharmaceutical-grade medical carbon dioxide for respiratory therapy, laparoscopic insufflation, cryosurgery, and cell culture. Available in three cylinder sizes, conforming to European Pharmacopeia standards with full GMP traceability.

use_cases.title: Respiratory Therapy
use_cases.description: Administered in controlled concentrations to stimulate breathing and alleviate respiratory acidosis, triggering the body's respiratory drive to overcome hypoventilation.

use_cases.title: Laparoscopic Surgery
use_cases.description: CO₂ insufflation creates the pneumoperitoneum required for laparoscopic and robotic procedures, providing a safe, non-flammable working cavity.

use_cases.title: Cryosurgery
use_cases.description: Liquid CO₂ powers cryosurgical probes for targeted, controlled tissue destruction in dermatological and gynaecological procedures.

use_cases.title: Cardiac & Vascular
use_cases.description: CO₂ angiography uses the gas as a contrast agent, enabling diagnostic imaging without nephrotoxic iodinated contrast — essential for renal-impaired patients.

use_cases.title: Cell Culture & Lab
use_cases.description: Maintains precise pH in CO₂ incubators for mammalian cell culture, essential in pharmaceutical R&D, IVF, and microbiology laboratories.

use_cases.title: Laser Surgery Assist
use_cases.description: Used as a purge and cooling gas in CO₂ laser surgical systems, improving beam quality and protecting optical components during precise tissue ablation.

SAFE USE
Handling & Storage
Store upright in ventilated areas
CO₂ is heavier than air — accumulation in low-lying or enclosed spaces creates an asphyxiation risk. Ensure continuous ventilation.
Temperature: 0 °C – 50 °C
Avoid prolonged direct sunlight or heat sources. CO₂ cylinders contain liquefied gas under high pressure — never expose to open flame.
Bull-nose valve — use matching regulator
All MCL medical CO₂ cylinders use the BS 341 bull-nose outlet. Ensure your pressure regulator is specifically rated for CO₂ service.
No oxygen enrichment risk
Unlike medical oxygen, CO₂ is non-flammable and non-oxidising. Standard conductive footwear and anti-static precautions still apply.
Colour code: Grey body (BS 4800)
MCL medical CO₂ cylinders are painted grey per international medical gas cylinder colour coding standards.

CLINICAL APPLICATIONS
01
Respiratory Therapy

Administered in controlled concentrations to stimulate breathing and alleviate respiratory acidosis, triggering the body's respiratory drive to overcome hypoventilation.

02
Laparoscopic Surgery

CO₂ insufflation creates the pneumoperitoneum required for laparoscopic and robotic procedures, providing a safe, non-flammable working cavity.

03
Cryosurgery

Liquid CO₂ powers cryosurgical probes for targeted, controlled tissue destruction in dermatological and gynaecological procedures.

04
Cardiac & Vascular

CO₂ angiography uses the gas as a contrast agent, enabling diagnostic imaging without nephrotoxic iodinated contrast — essential for renal-impaired patients.

05
Cell Culture & Lab

Maintains precise pH in CO₂ incubators for mammalian cell culture, essential in pharmaceutical R&D, IVF, and microbiology laboratories.

06
Laser Surgery Assist

Used as a purge and cooling gas in CO₂ laser surgical systems, improving beam quality and protecting optical components during precise tissue ablation.

WHERE IT'S USED
KEY APPLICATIONS
🔧
WELDING & METAL FABRICATION

Argon is the primary shielding gas for MIG and TIG welding, protecting the weld pool from atmospheric contamination and ensuring clean, porosity-free joints.

🏗
METAL PROCESSING

Inert argon atmospheres are used in heat treatment, annealing, and sintering of metals to prevent oxidation and surface scaling during high-temperature operations.

🧪
LABORATORY & R&D

Used as carrier gas in gas chromatography and as a plasma gas in inductively coupled plasma spectrometry for trace element analysis.

💡
LIGHTING INDUSTRY

Argon fills incandescent and fluorescent light bulbs, slowing filament evaporation and extending lamp life through its chemically inert properties.

⚗
CHEMICAL PROCESSING

Provides inert blankets over reactive chemical processes and is used in the production of specialty chemicals and reactive metals such as titanium.

🔬
SEMICONDUCTOR FABRICATION

Ultra-pure argon creates contamination-free environments for silicon wafer processing, thin-film deposition, and ion implantation in chip manufacturing.

WHERE IT'S USED
KEY APPLICATIONS
⚗
CHEMICAL PROCESSING

Hydrogen is a fundamental feedstock in the production of ammonia, methanol, and numerous chemical intermediates used across the petrochemical industry.

🏭
REFINING & HYDROTREATING

Oil refineries use hydrogen extensively for hydrotreating and hydrocracking processes to remove sulphur from fuels and upgrade heavier crude fractions.

🔩
METAL PROCESSING

Hydrogen atmospheres are used in the annealing and sintering of metals, providing a reducing environment that prevents oxidation and improves surface quality.

🪟
FLOAT GLASS MANUFACTURING

The float glass process uses a hydrogen and nitrogen atmosphere above the molten tin bath to prevent oxidation and produce flawless flat glass.

🧪
LABORATORY & R&D

Ultra-pure hydrogen serves as a carrier gas in gas chromatography and as a fuel gas for flame ionisation detectors in analytical laboratories.

⚡
FUEL CELLS & ENERGY

Hydrogen fuel cells convert hydrogen directly into electricity with water as the only byproduct, supporting clean energy and industrial power applications.

use_cases.title: MIG / MAG WELDING
use_cases.description: Ar/CO₂ blends are essential for MIG/MAG welding — CO₂ enhances penetration and arc energy while argon stabilises the arc and reduces spatter for cleaner, higher-quality welds.

use_cases.title: PARTICLE PHYSICS & RADIATION
use_cases.description: P10 gas (Ar/CH₄) fills proportional counters and multi-wire detectors used in nuclear research, radiation measurement, and high-energy particle physics experiments.

use_cases.title: CALIBRATION & METROLOGY
use_cases.description: Certified reference gas mixtures with traceable, certified compositions are used to calibrate gas analysers, environmental monitoring instruments, and industrial process sensors.

use_cases.title: DIVING & HYPERBARIC
use_cases.description: Heliox and trimix blends replace nitrogen in breathing gas for deep commercial diving, reducing narcosis risk and enabling operation at depths beyond air's safe limits.

use_cases.title: LABORATORY & RESEARCH
use_cases.description: Custom blends at precise concentrations support chromatography, spectroscopy, cell culture CO₂ incubation, and a wide range of other research and analytical applications.

use_cases.title: HEAT TREATMENT ATMOSPHERES
use_cases.description: Controlled N₂/H₂ and N₂/CH₄ mixtures create reducing or neutral atmospheres for annealing, sintering, and bright hardening of metals in industrial furnaces.

WHERE THEY'RE USED
KEY APPLICATIONS
🔥
MIG / MAG WELDING

Ar/CO₂ blends are essential for MIG/MAG welding — CO₂ enhances penetration and arc energy while argon stabilises the arc and reduces spatter for cleaner, higher-quality welds.

⚛
PARTICLE PHYSICS & RADIATION

P10 gas (Ar/CH₄) fills proportional counters and multi-wire detectors used in nuclear research, radiation measurement, and high-energy particle physics experiments.

🧪
CALIBRATION & METROLOGY

Certified reference gas mixtures with traceable, certified compositions are used to calibrate gas analysers, environmental monitoring instruments, and industrial process sensors.

🌊
DIVING & HYPERBARIC

Heliox and trimix blends replace nitrogen in breathing gas for deep commercial diving, reducing narcosis risk and enabling operation at depths beyond air's safe limits.

🔬
LABORATORY & RESEARCH

Custom blends at precise concentrations support chromatography, spectroscopy, cell culture CO₂ incubation, and a wide range of other research and analytical applications.

🏭
HEAT TREATMENT ATMOSPHERES

Controlled N₂/H₂ and N₂/CH₄ mixtures create reducing or neutral atmospheres for annealing, sintering, and bright hardening of metals in industrial furnaces.

WHERE IT'S USED
KEY APPLICATIONS
🧪
GAS CHROMATOGRAPHY

Helium is the preferred carrier gas in gas chromatography (GC and GC/MS), providing inert, high-diffusivity transport of analytes through capillary columns for precise analytical separations.

🏥
MRI CRYOCOOLING

Liquid helium cools the superconducting magnets in MRI scanners to near absolute zero (-269 °C), maintaining the magnetic field required for high-resolution diagnostic imaging.

🔍
LEAK DETECTION

Helium's small molecular size and atmospheric rarity make it the ideal tracer gas for mass spectrometer-based leak testing of vacuum systems, refrigeration units, and pressure vessels.

🌐
FIBRE OPTIC MANUFACTURING

High-purity helium purges the draw furnace and cooling tube during optical fibre drawing, preventing oxidation of the silica preform and ensuring low attenuation in the finished fibre.

⚡
SEMICONDUCTOR & ELECTRONICS

Used in epitaxial growth, ion implantation cooling, and as a backside cooling gas in wafer processing equipment to maintain precise temperature control during semiconductor fabrication.

🔥
TIG WELDING OF REACTIVE METALS

Helium additions to argon shielding gas increase arc temperature and heat input, enabling faster travel speeds when TIG welding thick aluminium, titanium, and copper alloys.
